evals.name: read-and-edit
evals.verdict: NEW WINS. New skill used --old/--new targeted edit. Old skill used full --content and suffered a concurrent edit collision.

evals.name: suggest-changes
evals.verdict: NEW WINS on behavior (used targeted --old/--new, handled conflict gracefully). Old skill faster only because it blindly piped full content. New skill's conflict detection prevented silent overwrite.

evals.name: file-management
evals.verdict: OLD WINS on tokens/time. New skill agent was slower — the folder already existed from the old skill run, causing complications. The auto-resolve root folder fix worked (no 400 errors) but the gotcha about --parent in the skill didn't fully prevent confusion.

key_findings.targeted_edit_adoption: v2 skill successfully guided agents to use --old/--new in both edit and suggest. v1 never did.
key_findings.conflict_safety: v2's targeted approach caught a concurrent edit (old string not found) instead of silently overwriting. v1's full replacement silently overwrote a concurrent change.
key_findings.file_management: Auto-resolve root folder eliminated 400 errors. But the new skill's gotchas section needs clearer guidance on create-doc --parent workflow.
key_findings.overall: v2 skill produces safer, more correct behavior (targeted edits, conflict detection). Token cost is similar or slightly higher due to more careful approach.
